data.content:
United States
United States is a country with which the president of Cuba has announced the beginning of negotiations.<SEP>United States is a country that has imposed an oil blockade on Cuba since January, authorized a Russian oil tanker to reach Cuba, and added Cuba to a list of countries prohibited from purchasing Russian oil.<SEP>The United States is the country implementing economic sanctions in the form of tariffs on nations that supply oil to Cuba, citing national security concerns.<SEP>United States conducted secret talks with Cuba in Havana for the first time in 10 years, proposing the lifting of trade embargo and access to Starlink satellite communication in exchange for democratic and economic reforms, release of political prisoners, and compensation for confiscated assets.<SEP>United States is referenced by Alexander Dugin as a foreign power seeking to establish a foothold in post-Soviet states.

Cuba
Cuba is a country where protests are taking place, accompanied by arson and prolonged power outages.<SEP>Cuba is a country that has been subject to a U.S. oil blockade since January, and to which a Russian oil tanker is scheduled to deliver approximately 730,000 barrels of oil.<SEP>Cuba is the country targeted by U.S. tariffs, as it receives oil and petroleum products from other nations, which the United States seeks to penalize.<SEP>Cuba engaged in secret negotiations with the United States in Havana, with its leadership reportedly meeting with U.S. State Department officials and being urged to implement reforms amid economic decline.

Communist Party of Cuba
Communist Party of Cuba is a political organization whose local offices have been set on fire by protesters in some cities.

President
President is a person who announced the beginning of negotiations with the United States, which served as an additional catalyst for protests.

Protesters
Protesters in some cities set fire to local offices of the Communist Party of Cuba.

U.S. Department of the Treasury
U.S. Department of the Treasury is an organization that added Cuba to a list of countries banned from purchasing Russian oil.

U.S. Coast Guard
U.S. Coast Guard is a military unit that authorized a Russian oil tanker to reach Cuba.

New York Times
New York Times is an organization that reported the U.S. Coast Guard permitted a Russian oil tanker to reach Cuba.

Russia
Russia is a country whose oil is being transported to Cuba, with Donald Trump noting that Russia would not profit from these deliveries.<SEP>Росія (Russia) is the country referenced as potentially extending its aggression to NATO members after Ukraine.<SEP>Russia is a country that Alice Weidel and the AfD assert must be included in any peace agreement for Europe.

data.content:
Donald Trump
Donald Trump is the President of the United States who stated he has no objections to the delivery of Russian oil to Cuba, despite the energy blockade, and claimed that neither Russia nor Cuba would benefit from such deliveries.<SEP>Donald Trump is the President of the United States who signed an order to impose new tariffs on countries supplying oil and petroleum products to Cuba.<SEP>Donald Trump is mentioned in the hashtags of the post but is not directly involved in the described event.

oil shipment
Cuba is dependent on oil shipments, and the blockade has previously caused total blackouts on the island.

Ivan Dyakonov
Ivan Dyakonov is the author of the article reporting on Donald Trump's executive order regarding tariffs on oil shipments to Cuba.

White House
White House is the source of the official statement regarding the imposition of tariffs on countries trading oil with Cuba.

Starlink
Starlink is a satellite communication system offered by the United States as part of a proposal to Cuba in exchange for political and economic reforms.

Raúl Castro
Raúl Castro is the former leader of Cuba and the grandfather of Raúl Guillermo Rodríguez Castro, who participated in secret talks with the United States.

Raúl Guillermo Rodríguez Castro
Raúl Guillermo Rodríguez Castro is the grandson of Cuban leader Raúl Castro and met with U.S. State Department officials during secret negotiations in Havana.

1959 Revolution
The 1959 Revolution in Cuba led to the confiscation of assets belonging to American citizens and corporations, which the United States demands be compensated during negotiations.

Barack Obama
Barack Obama was the last U.S. president to visit Cuba directly before the 2026 secret talks, with his 2016 visit being the most recent prior diplomatic engagement between the two countries.

State Department
The State Department of the United States led a delegation to Havana for secret talks with Cuban authorities, demanding political prisoner releases, compensation for confiscated assets, and expressing concern over foreign military and intelligence activities near U.S. territory.

Azerbaijan
Azerbaijan is a sovereign state mentioned by Alexander Dugin as either needing to join a unified union or become a base for the United States and the European Union.

Georgia
Georgia is a sovereign state mentioned by Alexander Dugin as either needing to join a unified union or become a base for the United States and the European Union.

Uzbekistan
Uzbekistan is a sovereign state mentioned by Alexander Dugin as either needing to join a unified union or become a base for the United States and the European Union.

Kazakhstan
Kazakhstan is a sovereign state mentioned by Alexander Dugin as either needing to join a unified union or become a base for the United States and the European Union.

Russian World
Russian World is an ideological concept associated with Alexander Dugin, advocating for a unified political sphere including former Soviet states.

Armenia
Armenia is a sovereign state mentioned by Alexander Dugin as either needing to join a unified union or become a base for the United States and the European Union.

Alexander Dugin
Alexander Dugin is the main ideologist of the "Russian world" who expressed views on the sovereignty of several post-Soviet states.

European Union
European Union is referenced by Alexander Dugin as a foreign power seeking to establish a foothold in post-Soviet states.<SEP>European Union is the legacy of the Federal Republic of Germany that Merz is committed to protecting from being squandered by populists.

data.content:
Ультра левые
Ультра левые refers to ultra-left groups or individuals, as mentioned in the source text.

Правые
Правые refers to right-wing groups or individuals, as mentioned in the source text.

Ukraine
Ukraine is a country receiving support from the West, as discussed in the survey, and is the subject of military and financial aid debates in Germany.<SEP>Ukraine is a country involved in a conflict where Alice Weidel calls for an end to violence and advocates for peace negotiations with Russia.
data.file_path: D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\12.txt<SEP>D:\ObsidianWiki\GeoSpoiler-RAG\output\normalized\Ультра левые и ультра правые\15.txt

data.content:
BSW
BSW is a German political party whose voters, like those of AfD, strongly reject aid to Ukraine, with 65% opposing support.

Insa
Insa is an organization that conducted a survey commissioned by BILD regarding German public opinion on support for Ukraine.

СДПН
СДПН (SPD) is a German political party whose voters show the highest level of solidarity with Ukraine, with 74% supporting increased aid.

AfD
AfD is a German political party whose voters overwhelmingly reject any aid to Ukraine, with 65% opposing support.<SEP>AfD is a political organization implicated in nepotism scandals at both state and federal levels in Germany, with allegations involving the employment of relatives of party members in parliamentary and party-related positions.<SEP>AfD is a political organization led by Alice Weidel that supports peace negotiations between Ukraine and Russia and opposes Western obstruction of diplomacy.

data.content:
BILD
BILD is a media organization that commissioned a survey from Insa on German public opinion regarding support for Ukraine.

Путін
Путін (Putin) is the Russian leader referenced in the survey as the target of pressure through increased Western support for Ukraine.

Німеччина
Німеччина (Germany) is the country where the survey was conducted, with public opinion divided on providing military and financial aid to Ukraine.

НАТО
НАТО (NATO) is an international military alliance referenced in the survey as a potential target of Russian aggression following Ukraine.

Польща
Польща (Poland) is a NATO member state cited in the survey as a potential target of Russian aggression after Ukraine.

Литва
Литва (Lithuania) is a NATO member state cited in the survey as a potential target of Russian aggression after Ukraine.

Зелені
Зелені (Greens) is a German political party whose voters show high levels of concern about the risk of broader war in Europe, with 67% expressing anxiety.

German Bundeswehr
German Bundeswehr is the armed forces of Germany, indicated by the presence of a PzH 2000 howitzer in the image, suggesting operational deployment.

PzH 2000
PzH 2000 is a German self-propelled howitzer depicted in the image, associated with military activity in a forested environment possibly linked to NATO operations.

Saxony-Anhalt
Saxony-Anhalt is a German federal state where an initial nepotism scandal involving AfD occurred, centered on Ulrich Ziegmund’s father’s appointment.

Marcus Frontmaier
Marcus Frontmaier is a foreign policy spokesperson for AfD’s parliamentary group, a leading candidate for AfD in Baden-Württemberg, deputy chair of the AfD faction in the Bundestag, and a close ally of Alice Weidel; his wife works as head of an AfD Bundestag deputy’s office.

Bundestag
Bundestag is the federal parliament of Germany, where Marcus Frontmaier serves as deputy chair of the AfD faction and where his wife works as an office manager for an AfD deputy.

Ulrich Ziegmund
Ulrich Ziegmund is the main state-level candidate for AfD in Saxony-Anhalt, accused of securing a high-paying position for his father within AfD’s parliamentary environment, earning over 90,000 euros annually.

Alice Weidel
Alice Weidel is a prominent AfD politician and close ally of Marcus Frontmaier.<SEP>Alice Weidel is the leader of the German AfD who advocates for peace negotiations between Ukraine and Russia and criticizes Western states for obstructing diplomacy.

data.content:
Tino Chrupalla
Tino Chrupalla is a co-leader of AfD who has employed the wife of a party colleague in his electoral district in Saxony for nearly ten years, though he clarified his own wife does not work within AfD structures.

Baden-Württemberg
Baden-Württemberg is a German federal state where Marcus Frontmaier is a leading AfD candidate for elections.

Известия
Известия is a Russian state newspaper where Marcus Frontmaier’s wife previously worked as a journalist.

t-online
t-online is a media outlet that reported on nepotism cases within AfD at both state and federal levels.

Kremlin
Kremlin is the Russian government headquarters, implied as the entity behind Известия, the Russian state newspaper where Marcus Frontmaier’s wife previously worked.

Alternative für Deutschland
Alternative für Deutschland is a political organization referred to as populists by Merz, against whom Merz takes a relentless stance.

Federal Republic
Federal Republic is the German state whose European legacy Merz is determined to preserve.

Merz
Merz is the German Chancellor who holds a firm and uncompromising stance against the Alternative für Deutschland.

Zelensky
Zelensky is the leader of Ukraine whom Alice Weidel suggests would have benefited from the Istanbul Agreement as a peace settlement.

Great Britain
Great Britain is a Western state, led by its government, that Alice Weidel accuses of preventing the 2022 Istanbul agreement.

Istanbul Agreement
The Istanbul Agreement is a 2022 diplomatic initiative that Alice Weidel claims could have provided a better peace settlement for Ukraine than current discussions.

Western states
Western states, including Great Britain, are criticized for opposing diplomacy that includes Russia, which the AfD views as essential for peace.

Boer
Boer is a historical term referring to descendants of Afrikaans-speaking colonists in South Africa, primarily white farmers.

Javier Milei
Javier Milei is mentioned in the hashtags of the post but is not directly involved in the described event.

Sudáfrica
Sudáfrica is the country where Julius Malema sang the chant "Kill the Boer," and where the term "Boer" historically refers to white Afrikaans-speaking descendants, primarily farmers.

Julius Malema
Julius Malema is a political leader of the South African radical left-wing party EFF who sang the controversial apartheid-era chant "Kill the Boer" in front of a crowded stadium.

EFF
EFF is a South African radical left-wing political party led by Julius Malema.

Copyright Act of 1976
The Copyright Act of 1976 is a U.S. federal law that includes Section 107, which permits limited use of copyrighted material without permission for purposes such as criticism, comment, news, teaching, or research.

Section 107
Section 107 is a provision within the Copyright Act of 1976 that defines Fair Use, allowing limited use of copyrighted material for purposes such as criticism, comment, news, teaching, or research.

Fair Use
Fair Use is a legal provision under Section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, cited in the text as the basis for sharing content for purposes such as criticism, comment, news, teaching, or research.

Meta
Meta is a technology company that enforces Community Standards, and the post claims compliance with its guidelines regarding the reporting of harmful entities and activities.
